_comment: SYNTHETIC / MOCK internal-moat output. Stands in for a query against Quiver's real fused EP-CRISPR latent space. s_internal, internal_id, provenance, and neighborhood_density are AUTHORED (clearly labeled MOCK). The corroboration sub-scores (genetics/ppi/screen) are transcribed from REAL EMET evidence: L3 corroboration chat d070bf32 (43 sources) and L2 Drug Safety chat eacfaebc. Disease-gene anchors SCN10A (Nav1.8) and SCN9A (Nav1.7) are EXCLUDED as already-prosecuted; the cascade's value is promoting a non-obvious target.

candidates.moat_note: Capsaicin/heat receptor — large, sharp EP current; moat ranks it #1.
candidates.gate_evidence: Class-wide on-target HYPERTHERMIA (mean +0.76 to +1.8 C); every systemic Phase 2 program failed (AMG-517, SB-705498, JNJ-39439335). [L2: PMID 31926897, 38492574, 35968676]

candidates.moat_note: alpha2-delta-1 VGCC subunit — strong Ca-current modulation signal.
candidates.gate_evidence: FDA black-box (2019) respiratory depression; 267K/360K FAERS reports; 4,569 fatal pregabalin outcomes; Schedule V abuse liability. On-target, ubiquitous CNS expression. [L2: PMID 28972983, 28493329; FDA 2019]

candidates.moat_note: Irritant-evoked current — strong EP signature; genuinely strong target.
candidates.gate_evidence: Broadly-tuned chemosensor expressed beyond nociceptors (airway, vascular) -> systemic selectivity concern. [L3: FEPS1 N855S; broad hub edges]

candidates.moat_note: ATP-gated current in C-fibres — strong EP signature.
candidates.gate_evidence: Dose-limiting dysgeusia 58-69% at 45mg BID (gefapixant COUGH-1/2); FDA CRL 2023; no life-threatening tox. Primary validated indication is cough, not pain. [L2: PMID 35248186, 36879087, 40430576]

candidates.moat_note: NGF ligand — strong CRISPR-perturbation signal (KO collapses nociceptor program).
candidates.gate_evidence: Anti-NGF (tanezumab/fasinumab) -> rapidly progressive osteoarthritis (2-7%), osteonecrosis, joint replacement; FDA holds 2010/2012; NDA rejected 2021. [L2: PMID 37652258, 26554876; NCT02697773]

candidates.moat_note: Kv7.2 M-current — EP signature, but primarily a seizure/DEE gene.
candidates.gate_evidence: Kv7 opener retigabine/ezogabine WITHDRAWN worldwide 2017 (blue pigmentation, retinal toxicity, urinary retention); CNS seizure-gene context. [L2: PMID 25642319; FDA 2013]

candidates.moat_note: Nav1.9: ultra-slow, non-inactivating PERSISTENT Na+ current is hard to resolve in the optical-EP assay -> the moat UNDER-DETECTS it and ranks it #7. EMET independently notes its 'ultra-slow kinetic profile.' THIS IS THE TARGET TO WATCH.
candidates.gate_evidence: Most favorable safety profile in the panel: restricted peripheral expression (low CNS), GoF genetics validate analgesia, LoF tolerable (pain insensitivity WITHOUT anhidrosis/autonomic failure, unlike CIPA). No clinical inhibitor toxicities on record. [L2: PMID 26243570, 26746779, 31551682]

candidates.moat_note: TrkA receptor tyrosine kinase — no direct channel EP signature; indirect/transcriptional excitability signal only.
candidates.gate_evidence: CIPA (complete LoF) phenotype predicts anhidrosis (life-threatening hyperthermia), bone fragility, sympathetic failure; shares anti-NGF RPOA liability; CNS-penetrant TRK effects. [L2: PMID 37869783, 26554876]

candidates.moat_note: Developmental transcription factor — no effector EP signature.
candidates.gate_evidence: No safety veto, BUT a developmental regulator, not a druggable adult-pain effector (EMET: 'not a pharmacological target in adult pain'); no direct STRING edge to SCN10A. -> uncertainty/abstention candidate.

expected_final_ranking.note: #7 -> #1: SAFE + strong Mendelian genetics; moat under-resolved its persistent current
